name: Stimulant & Adenosine Sensitivity
description: Variants controlling how caffeine is preferred, metabolized, and experienced in the brain.

snps.description: T allele associated with higher caffeine-induced anxiety.

TT.note: High anxiety sensitivity to caffeine — moderate intake.

snps.description: Companion marker to rs762551; further modulates caffeine metabolism rate.
Cross-referenced from methylation panel; modulates subjective stimulant response via prefrontal dopamine.

GG.note: Warrior — dopamine clears fast; stimulants may feel less intense.

AA.note: Worrier — stimulants may feel stronger, potentially more anxiogenic.

snps.description: Intergenic marker near CYP1A1/CYP1A2 associated with slightly higher habitual coffee consumption.

CC.note: No common higher-coffee-intake allele detected at this site.

CT.note: One higher-coffee-intake allele is present. Habitual caffeine use may drift a bit higher than baseline.

TT.note: Two higher-coffee-intake alleles are present. This is a stronger common tendency toward higher coffee intake.
